description: BitReader.skip_bits() avoids redundant fill_buffer after peek. HuffmanTable uses Box<[LookupEntry;256]> instead of Vec. #[inline(always)] on lookup, #[cold] on slow path, start slow path at min_slow_length. Branchless extend(). Eliminated format!() in error paths.
Fast-path read_bits: skip fill_buffer when bits_left >= count (common after peek+skip). Saves one comparison per read_bits call.

Infallible BitReader: peek_bits/read_bits return u16 directly (no Result wrapping). EOF returns 0. Eliminates 2 error-path branches per AC coefficient decode iteration. Also #[inline(never)] on lookup_slow.
Attempted fast 4-byte bulk fill in BitReader (check 4 bytes for 0xFF, load all at once if clean). No improvement — branch predictor already handles per-byte 0xFF check well. Reverted.
9-bit Huffman fast-lookup table (512 entries, was 256). Catches more AC codes on fast path. 10-bit tested but worse (1024 entries hurts L1 cache).

Infallible lookup_fast() for AC decode: returns raw (u8,u8) tuple, no Result. AC loop uses it for fast path, falls back to lookup() for long codes. Eliminates Result discriminant from hot inner loop. DC fast-path tested but HURT perf (code layout change confused branch predictor) — kept DC using lookup().
